tasks.title: Initialize React + Vite + Tailwind project
tasks.description: Create the foundational frontend project structure using Vite with React and configure Tailwind CSS
tasks.details: Run `npm create vite@latest . -- --template react-ts` to initialize project with TypeScript. Install Tailwind CSS: `npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer`, then `npx tailwindcss init -p`. Configure tailwind.config.js with content paths: ['./index.html', './src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}']. Add Tailwind directives to index.css: @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;. Set up base folder structure: src/components, src/pages, src/lib, src/types, src/styles.
tasks.testStrategy: Run `npm run dev` and verify the Vite dev server starts successfully. Check that Tailwind classes render correctly by adding a test component with Tailwind utilities.

tasks.title: Configure Supabase project and environment variables
tasks.description: Set up Supabase project instance and configure environment variables for all required services
tasks.details: Create new Supabase project at supabase.com. Copy project URL and anon key. Create .env file based on .env.example with: VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (for Edge Functions), ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (for Claude API), RESEND_API_KEY (for email). Install Supabase client: `npm install @supabase/supabase-js`. Create src/lib/supabase.ts with createClient configuration using environment variables.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all environment variables are properly loaded. Test Supabase connection by running a simple query to check connectivity in browser console.

tasks.title: Create database schema and migrations
tasks.description: Design and implement the complete PostgreSQL database schema with all tables and relationships
tasks.details:
Create Supabase migration file with SQL schema:

-- teams table: id (uuid/pk), name, city, slug (unique), abbreviation, primary_color, secondary_color, accent_color, logo_url, division, conference, is_active (default false), created_at

-- subscribers table: id (uuid/pk), email (unique), team_id (fk → teams), subscribed_at (default now()), is_active (default true), last_opened_at, created_at

-- sources table: id (uuid/pk), team_id (nullable fk → teams), url (unique), name, type (enum: global/team_specific/user_submitted), status (enum: pending/approved/rejected/flagged), relevance_score (0-100), submitted_by (email), created_at, updated_at

-- articles table: id (uuid/pk), source_id (fk → sources), team_id (fk → teams), title, original_url (unique), raw_content (text), ai_summary, published_at, relevance_confirmed (boolean), category (enum: transaction/injury/game_analysis/rumor/general), created_at

-- newsletters table: id (uuid/pk), team_id (fk → teams), sent_at, subject_line, html_content (text), status (enum: draft/sent), created_at

-- newsletter_metrics table: id (uuid/pk), newsletter_id (fk → newsletters), subscriber_id (fk → subscribers), opened_at, feedback (enum: thumbs_up/thumbs_down, nullable), created_at

Add indexes on: subscribers.email, sources.status, articles.team_id + published_at, newsletters.team_id + sent_at. Enable RLS on all tables.
tasks.testStrategy: Run migration in Supabase dashboard. Verify all tables created with correct columns, data types, constraints, and indexes. Test foreign key relationships.

tasks.title: Design HTML email template structure
tasks.description: Create responsive, mobile-first email template with four content sections
tasks.details:
Create src/email-templates/newsletter.ts with HTML structure:

- DOCTYPE and HTML5 boilerplate for email
- Head: meta viewport, title, inline CSS (email clients strip external CSS)
- Body: full-width table-based layout (for email client compatibility)
- Header: NFL Wire logo (centered), team logo, team colors as accent
- Section 1 - Top Story: article title (24px, bold), 3-sentence summary, source attribution (gray, 12px), timestamp, Read More link (team primary color)
- Section 2 - Quick Hits: 3 articles, each with headline (18px, bold), 2-sentence summary, source
- Section 3 - Injury Report: table with columns (Player, Position, Injury, Status), color-coded status (Out=red, Questionable=yellow, Probable=green)
- Section 4 - Stat of the Day: large stat text (36px, team accent color), 2-sentence context
- Footer: 👍 / 👎 feedback buttons (large tap targets 44x44px), Unsubscribe link, Submit a source link
- Typography: Use web-safe fonts as fallback (Georgia for serif, Arial for sans)
- Mobile-first: single column, 600px max width, generous padding (20px), font size >= 16px for readability
- Team colors: use as border accents, button backgrounds, headings

Export as template function accepting { team, articles, stat }.
tasks.testStrategy: Render template with test data in browser. Test responsive behavior (mobile, tablet, desktop). Validate HTML using W3C validator. Check accessibility (alt text, semantic HTML).

tasks.title: Set up Supabase Auth for admin access
tasks.description: Configure authentication and role-based access control for admin dashboard
tasks.details:
Enable Supabase Auth in project. Configure email auth provider. Create admin user manually in Supabase dashboard. Add custom claim for admin role:

-- Create admin_users table
CREATE TABLE admin_users (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
  user_id uuid REFERENCES auth.users(id),
  email text UNIQUE,
  created_at timestamp DEFAULT now()
);

-- Insert admin user(s)
INSERT INTO admin_users (user_id, email) VALUES ('[AUTH-USER-ID]', 'admin@nflwire.com');

-- Create RLS policy to check admin access
CREATE POLICY "Admin access only" ON [TABLE-NAME]
  FOR ALL
  USING (EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM admin_users
    WHERE admin_users.user_id = auth.uid()
  ));

Install auth helpers: `npm install @supabase/auth-helpers-react`. Create src/lib/auth.tsx with AuthProvider and useAuth hook. Create src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx that checks isAdmin before rendering admin pages.

tasks.details:

const { data: sources } = await supabase
  .from('sources')
  .select('*, teams(name)')
  .in('status', ['pending', 'flagged'])
  .order('created_at', { ascending: false });

Display in table:

// 5-of-7 weekly engagement (north star metric)

const subscribersWhoOpened5Plus = [...]; // Calculate per subscriber

// Satisfaction rate

  .from('newsletter_metrics')

tasks.title: Phase 3 v2: Fetch full article bodies (not just RSS)
tasks.description: Update the content pipeline to fetch and extract full article text for each candidate item before summarization/scoring
tasks.details:
PRD update: the pipeline must fetch full article body content from each RSS item link (original_url), not rely solely on RSS <description> snippets.

Implementation notes:
- In `supabase/functions/run-content-pipeline/index.ts`, after parsing RSS items, fetch each `link` (with timeouts, content-type checks, and retry once) and extract readable text.
- Use deterministic extraction (strip scripts/styles, basic readability heuristics). Cap extracted text length (e.g. 8k-12k chars) to control cost.
- Preserve the RSS title/link/published_at as metadata; store extracted body as the source of truth for downstream steps.
- Ensure idempotency/dedupe still works on `original_url`.

Edge cases:
- Paywalls / blocked bots / 403: skip and record reason in pipeline response.
- Non-HTML articles (PDF/video pages): skip.
- Timeouts: skip and record.

Security/perf:
- Do not execute remote JS. Only fetch + parse HTML.
- Respect strict timeouts and max articles per source/team.
tasks.testStrategy: Run pipeline and verify `sources_tested` shows RSS items found, and that each accepted article has extracted body text length > 0. Confirm no article is summarized/scored without extracted body content.

tasks.title: Phase 3 v2: Add AI selection scoring with composite threshold
tasks.description: Score each candidate article on relevance/significance/credibility/uniqueness and compute composite_score with threshold >= 65
tasks.details:
PRD update: score articles using Claude on the fetched full article body only (not RSS snippets).

Requirements:
- Per article, compute numeric subscores: relevance, significance, credibility, uniqueness (0-100).
- Compute `composite_score` (document formula; default average unless PRD specifies weights).
- Only allow articles with `composite_score >= 65` to proceed into ranking/selection.
- Avoid double-counting duplicates: if uniqueness is low, composite should fall below threshold.

Implementation notes:
- Add a scoring step between filtering and summarization (or combine summarization + scoring in one Claude call, returning structured JSON).
- Validate JSON with strict schema (Zod) before storing.
- Keep temperature low (0.2-0.4).
tasks.testStrategy: Run pipeline on a day with mixed items. Verify each article has subscores + composite, and that articles below 65 are excluded from lead/quick hits. Confirm scoring uses extracted body only.

tasks.title: Phase 3 v2: Store selection reasoning per article
tasks.description: Persist model-produced selection_reasoning explaining why each kept article was included (and optionally why exclusions happened)
tasks.details:
PRD update: store `selection_reasoning` for each article selected into the newsletter set.

Implementation notes:
- Extend storage to include `selection_reasoning` text for selected articles.
- The reasoning must reference the scoring dimensions and the key facts from the fetched article body.
- Do not store reasoning for articles that were never fetched/extracted.

This likely requires a DB migration (new column on `articles` or a separate `article_scores` / `article_selection` table).
tasks.testStrategy: Run pipeline and confirm selected articles in DB include non-empty `selection_reasoning` that matches the scoring output and does not cite facts absent from the body.

tasks.details:

- Define what qualifies as 'suspicious' (e.g. numbers, records, dates, transactional claims) and extract candidate claims from the fetched body.
- For suspicious claims, do a web check (search + fetch) and either confirm, soften language, or exclude the claim.
- Store validation outcome (confirmed/uncertain/false) and sources used for validation (URLs).

Note: This may require new DB fields/tables to store validation results.
tasks.testStrategy: Inject a known false/suspicious claim in a controlled test article and verify the pipeline flags it and adjusts output accordingly, recording validation sources and outcome.

tasks.title: Phase 3 v2: Generate newsletter content strictly from fetched data
tasks.description: Ensure lead story, quick hits, injuries, stat of the day, and summaries only use fetched/extracted article content
tasks.details:
PRD update: generate newsletter content from fetched data only.

Implementation notes:
- Summaries must be grounded strictly in extracted article bodies.
- Stat of the day must either be derived from the extracted data or omitted when not verifiable.
- Ensure no 'general knowledge' fill unless explicitly allowed by PRD.

This task should also update API responses/tests to prove grounding (e.g. include per-article source URLs and optional citations for validated facts).
tasks.testStrategy: Run pipeline and confirm every generated text block can be traced back to extracted body content and/or validated sources; no invented stats.

tasks.title: Implement Duplicate Suppression for Article Selection
tasks.description: Before article selection, query the newsletters table for all previously published article URLs and exclude them from consideration. Collapse same-event stories from multiple sources down to the single highest composite_score source using both URL exact match and semantic title similarity above 80%.
tasks.testStrategy:
**1. URL Exact-Match Exclusion**
- Insert a test article URL into a mock "sent" newsletter's `article_urls` column for a given team.
- Run the pipeline for that team and verify the article with the matching URL does not appear in the candidate list passed to `rankAndSelectArticles`.
- Verify articles with different URLs are not excluded.

**2. Semantic Title Similarity Exclusion (Previously Published)**
- Insert a published article title like "Seahawks sign wide receiver to 3-year deal" into a sent newsletter's `article_titles`.
- Introduce a new candidate article with title "Seahawks sign WR to three-year contract" (should score ≥ 80% similarity).
- Verify the new candidate is excluded.
- Introduce a candidate with a clearly different title and verify it is NOT excluded.

**3. Cross-Source Same-Event Collapse**
- Create 3 candidate articles covering the same event (similar titles, ≥ 80% similarity) from 3 different sources with composite_scores of 0.9, 0.7, and 0.5.
- Run `collapseDuplicates` and verify only the article with score 0.9 is retained.
- Verify the other two are removed.
- Create 2 candidate articles with clearly different titles and verify both are retained.

**4. Dice Coefficient Unit Tests**
- Test `diceCoefficient("seahawks sign receiver", "seahawks sign receiver")` → 1.0.
- Test `diceCoefficient("seahawks sign receiver", "completely unrelated story")` → well below 0.80.
- Test boundary cases: empty strings, single-word titles.

**5. End-to-End Pipeline Test**
- Seed the database with a sent newsletter containing 3 article URLs and titles for the Seahawks.
- Fetch 10 new articles, 3 of which are duplicates (1 exact URL match, 2 near-title matches), and 2 of which are same-event duplicates from different sources.
- Run the full pipeline and verify: (a) the 3 previously published articles are excluded, (b) only the higher-scored article from each same-event pair survives, (c) the final candidate pool passed to ranking contains only unique, novel articles.

**6. Schema Validation**
- Verify `article_urls` and `article_titles` columns exist on the `newsletters` table.
- Verify newsletter assembly (Task 26) correctly populates both columns when a newsletter is assembled.

**7. Configuration Test**
- Set `DUPLICATE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD=0.95` and verify that articles with 85% title similarity are no longer excluded.
- Set `DUPLICATE_LOOKBACK_DAYS=1` and verify articles from newsletters older than 1 day are not considered published duplicates.

tasks.title: Implement Empty Pipeline Fallback and Reduced Newsletter Logic
tasks.description: Add graceful degradation logic to the content pipeline so that zero-article runs skip sending and notify the admin, 1–2 article runs produce a reduced "light-day" newsletter with a header notice, and any section with no content is omitted from the rendered HTML entirely.
tasks.testStrategy:
## 1. Zero-Article Fallback

- Mock `scoredArticles` to return an empty array (or all articles with `composite_score < COMPOSITE_SCORE_THRESHOLD`).
- Invoke the pipeline for a test team.
- **Assert:** No email is sent (verify SendGrid/Resend call count = 0).
- **Assert:** A `newsletters` row is inserted with `status = 'skipped'` and `skip_reason = 'no_qualified_articles'`.
- **Assert:** Admin notification email is received with the correct team ID and reason message.
- **Assert:** Pipeline returns `{ status: 'skipped', reason: 'no_qualified_articles' }`.
- **Assert:** Structured log line `[pipeline:fallback] … action=skip` appears in Supabase function logs.

## 2. Light-Day Fallback (1 Article)

- Mock `scoredArticles` to return exactly 1 article above threshold.
- Run the pipeline.
- **Assert:** Email IS sent.
- **Assert:** The rendered HTML contains the `light-day-notice` banner with the expected copy.
- **Assert:** Only sections with content are present in the HTML (e.g., no `<section>` for quick hits or injuries if those arrays are empty).
- **Assert:** `newsletters` row has `status = 'sent'` (not skipped).

## 3. Light-Day Fallback (2 Articles)

- Repeat test 2 with exactly 2 articles.
- **Assert:** Same light-day banner present.
- **Assert:** Sections that have content render; sections without content are absent.

## 4. Full Newsletter (≥3 Articles)

- Mock `scoredArticles` to return 5 articles above threshold.
- **Assert:** No light-day banner in rendered HTML.
- **Assert:** All populated sections render normally.

## 5. Empty Section Omission

- Provide articles that produce a lead story and quick hits but no injury items and no stat.
- **Assert:** Injuries section HTML is absent from the output.
- **Assert:** Stat section HTML is absent from the output.
- **Assert:** No empty `<section>` or placeholder tags appear anywhere in the HTML.

## 6. Threshold Boundary

- Test an article with `composite_score` exactly equal to `COMPOSITE_SCORE_THRESHOLD`.
- **Assert:** Article IS included (boundary is inclusive `>=`).
- Test an article with `composite_score` one epsilon below threshold.
- **Assert:** Article is excluded.

## 7. Admin Notification Content

- Trigger the zero-article path.
- Inspect the admin email: verify it contains team name/ID, the threshold value, and a timestamp.
- Verify the email does NOT block or delay the pipeline response (fire-and-forget / non-blocking).

## 8. Database Schema

- Run `\d newsletters` and confirm `skip_reason` column exists.
- Confirm `status = 'skipped'` is accepted without constraint violation.

tasks.title: Enforce Explicit Composite Score Formula with Separate Dimension Storage and Adaptive Threshold
tasks.description: Implement the explicit weighted composite score formula — (relevance × 0.40) + (significance × 0.30) + (credibility × 0.20) + (uniqueness × 0.10) — storing all four dimension scores as separate columns in the articles table, and apply a standard threshold of 65 (lowered to 55 under low-volume conditions).
tasks.testStrategy:
## 1. Unit Tests – `computeCompositeScore`

- **Exact formula verification:** Call `computeCompositeScore({ relevance: 100, significance: 100, credibility: 100, uniqueness: 100 })` and assert result equals `100`.
- **Weighted correctness:** Call with `{ relevance: 80, significance: 60, credibility: 70, uniqueness: 50 }` and assert result equals `(80×0.40)+(60×0.30)+(70×0.20)+(50×0.10)` = `32+18+14+5` = **69.0**.
- **Zero case:** All zeros should return `0`.
- **Weight sum sanity:** Assert `Object.values(SCORE_WEIGHTS).reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0) === 1.0`.

## 2. Unit Tests – `resolveThreshold`

- With `candidateCount = 10` (≥ LOW_VOLUME_ARTICLE_COUNT), assert threshold = **65**.
- With `candidateCount = 3` (< LOW_VOLUME_ARTICLE_COUNT), assert threshold = **55**.
- With `candidateCount = LOW_VOLUME_ARTICLE_COUNT - 1`, assert threshold = **55**.
- With `candidateCount = LOW_VOLUME_ARTICLE_COUNT`, assert threshold = **65**.

## 3. Database Migration Tests

- After running the migration, query `information_schema.columns` for the `articles` table and assert all four columns exist: `score_relevance`, `score_significance`, `score_credibility`, `score_uniqueness`.
- Assert each column has type `NUMERIC` and allows NULL.
- Assert `composite_score` column exists.

## 4. Integration Tests – Scoring Pipeline

- Insert a test article with known body content into the `articles` table.
- Run the scoring step against it (mock Claude to return `{ relevance: 70, significance: 50, credibility: 80, uniqueness: 40 }`).
- Assert the article row in `articles` has:
  - `score_relevance = 70`
  - `score_significance = 50`
  - `score_credibility = 80`
  - `score_uniqueness = 40`
  - `composite_score = (70×0.40)+(50×0.30)+(80×0.20)+(40×0.10)` = `28+15+16+4` = **63.0**
- Assert this article is **excluded** under the standard threshold (63 < 65).
- Assert this article is **included** under the low-volume threshold (63 ≥ 55).

## 5. Integration Tests – Threshold Gating

- Prepare a set of 10 articles with composite scores ranging from 40 to 90.
- Run the full pipeline; assert only articles with `composite_score >= 65` are passed to `rankAndSelectArticles`.
- Reduce the candidate set to 3 articles (all scoring between 55–64); assert all three pass through under the low-volume threshold.
- Reduce the candidate set to 3 articles with scores below 55; assert all are excluded even under low-volume threshold.

## 6. Malformed Claude Response Handling

- Mock Claude to return an incomplete JSON (e.g. missing `uniqueness`).
- Assert the pipeline does not crash; the missing dimension defaults to `0` and a warning is logged.
- Assert `composite_score` is computed using `0` for the missing dimension.

## 7. Backward Compatibility

- Query articles with `NULL` dimension scores; assert they are treated as unscored and re-scored on the next run (not erroneously included or excluded based on stale data).

## 8. Logging Verification

- Run the pipeline in a test environment and capture stdout.
- Assert log lines contain `[score] id=... R=... S=... C=... U=... composite=...` for each article.
- Assert a summary log line contains `threshold=`, `candidates=`, and `qualified=` values.

tasks.title: Implement Mid-Game Article Detection and Holding Logic
tasks.description: Detect articles fetched during an active game window by evaluating article timestamps and live-game keywords, flag them, hold them from newsletter inclusion, and re-evaluate 3 hours after the scheduled game end before allowing them into the pipeline.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Unit Tests

### 1. `detectMidGameArticle` — Keyword Detection
- Create a mock article with `title: "Live Q3 Update: Seahawks vs 49ers"` and a `gameWindow` covering `published_at`. Verify `isMidGame: true` and `matchedKeywords` includes `'live'` and `'q3'`.
- Create an article with `title: "Seahawks sign new linebacker"` published during a game window. Verify `isMidGame: true` even without keywords (timestamp-only flag).
- Create an article published 5 hours before game start. Verify `isMidGame: false`.
- Pass `gameWindow: null` for any article. Verify `isMidGame: false`.

### 2. `getActiveGameWindow` — Schedule Lookup
- Insert a test game row with `scheduled_start = now - 1 hour`, `scheduled_end = now + 2 hours`. Call `getActiveGameWindow` and verify it returns the game.
- Insert a game that ended 2 hours ago. Verify `null` is returned (outside buffer).
- Insert a future game starting in 1 hour. Verify `null` is returned.

---

## Integration Tests

### 3. Pipeline Holding Behavior
- Seed a game in `game_schedule` for the test team covering the current time window.
- Seed two articles: one with `title: "Halftime Report: Seahawks lead 14-7"` (published now) and one with `title: "Seahawks injury report"` (published yesterday).
- Run `run-content-pipeline` for the test team.
- Verify the halftime article has `status = 'held_midgame'` in the `articles` table.
- Verify a row exists in `held_articles` with `status = 'held'` and `re_evaluate_at = scheduled_game_end + 3 hours`.
- Verify the injury report article (published outside game window) passes through normally.
- Verify `rankAndSelectArticles` is called only with non-held articles.

### 4. Re-Evaluation — Release Path
- Insert a `held_articles` row with `re_evaluate_at = now - 1 minute`, `status = 'held'`, and no active game window for the team.
- Trigger `re-evaluate-held-articles` function manually.
- Verify the `held_articles` row is updated to `status = 'released'`.
- Verify the corresponding `articles` row has `status = 'active'` and `hold_reason = null`.

### 5. Re-Evaluation — Game Still Active (Extend Hold)
- Insert a `held_articles` row with `re_evaluate_at = now - 1 minute` but ensure an active game window still exists for the team.
- Trigger `re-evaluate-held-articles`.
- Verify `re_evaluate_at` is extended by 1 hour and `status` remains `'held'`.

### 6. Admin Notification
- Run the pipeline with held articles and verify `notifyAdmin` is called with the correct held article count and titles.

### 7. Edge Cases
- Run pipeline when no game is scheduled — verify zero articles are held.
- Run pipeline with an article containing no keywords but published exactly at `scheduled_start` — verify it is flagged.
- Verify that articles already in `held_articles` with `status = 'released'` are not re-held on subsequent pipeline runs (check for duplicate insert guard).

tasks.title: Implement Paywall and Fetch Quality Detection in Content Pipeline
tasks.description: After fetching full article bodies, validate a minimum of 200 words and detect paywall/boilerplate indicators. Discard flagged articles, track paywall rates per source, and surface sources exceeding 30% paywall rate in the admin Source Queue.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Unit Tests

### Word Count Validation
- Pass a string with exactly 199 words → verify article is discarded with reason `insufficient_content`.
- Pass a string with exactly 200 words → verify article proceeds to scoring.
- Pass an empty string → verify article is discarded.

### Paywall Detection
- Pass body text containing "Subscribe to continue reading" → verify `detectPaywall` returns `true`.
- Pass body text containing "Sign in to access this article" → verify `detectPaywall` returns `true`.
- Pass body text containing "We use cookies to improve your experience. Accept all cookies." → verify `detectBoilerplate` returns `true`.
- Pass a clean 300-word article body → verify both functions return `false`.

### HTTP Status Handling
- Mock a fetch response with HTTP 402 → verify article is discarded and logged as `paywall_detected`.
- Mock a fetch response with HTTP 401 → verify article is discarded and logged as `paywall_detected`.

---

## Integration Tests

### Pipeline Discard Flow
1. Insert a test source with a known paywalled URL (e.g., a URL that returns paywall HTML).
2. Run the `run-content-pipeline` function for that source's team.
3. Verify no article from that URL appears in the `articles` table.
4. Verify a row exists in `fetch_quality_logs` with `discard_reason = 'paywall_detected'` for that URL.

### Paywall Rate Calculation
1. Insert 10 `fetch_quality_logs` rows for a source: 4 with `paywall_detected`, 6 with successful fetches (simulate by inserting non-discard rows or tracking total attempts).
2. Call `updateSourcePaywallRate` for that source.
3. Verify `sources.paywall_rate` is updated to `0.4` (40%).
4. Verify `sources.status` is set to `'flagged'`.
5. Verify admin notification email is sent with the correct paywall rate.

### Below-Threshold Source
1. Insert 10 fetch logs for a source: 2 paywall, 8 successful.
2. Call `updateSourcePaywallRate`.
3. Verify `paywall_rate = 0.2` and `status` is NOT changed to `'flagged'`.

### Insufficient Sample Exemption
1. Insert only 3 fetch logs for a source, all `paywall_detected`.
2. Call `updateSourcePaywallRate`.
3. Verify source is NOT auto-flagged despite 100% paywall rate (fewer than 5 attempts).

---

## Admin Source Queue Tests
1. Set a source's `paywall_rate` to `0.35` and `status` to `'flagged'` in the database.
2. Open the admin Source Queue view.
3. Verify the source appears with a red "Paywall Rate: 35%" badge.
4. Verify the source is sorted to the top of the flagged list.
5. Verify the Approve/Reject actions still work correctly for paywall-flagged sources.

---

## End-to-End Test
1. Configure a test source pointing to a URL that returns a paywall page.
2. Trigger a full pipeline run.
3. Confirm: article discarded → log created → source paywall_rate updated → (if >30% with ≥5 samples) source flagged → admin email received → Source Queue shows flagged source with paywall rate.

tasks.title: Implement Summary Freshness Check with Generic Language Detection
tasks.description: After generating each article summary, call the Claude API to detect generic language that could apply to any team or any week, and regenerate the summary with explicit specificity instructions if generic phrases are detected. Store an `is_generic_summary` boolean flag in the articles table to track quality.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Unit Tests

### 1. Generic Detection — True Positive
- Pass a clearly generic summary like `"The team is preparing for their upcoming game and players are working hard in practice."` with team `"Seattle Seahawks"` and a date.
- Verify `isSummaryGeneric` returns `{ isGeneric: true, reason: <non-empty string> }`.

### 2. Generic Detection — True Negative
- Pass a specific summary like `"Geno Smith completed 28 of 38 passes for 312 yards as the Seattle Seahawks defeated the 49ers 30-23 on Sunday."` 
- Verify `isSummaryGeneric` returns `{ isGeneric: false }`.

### 3. Regeneration Quality
- Trigger regeneration for a known generic summary.
- Verify the regenerated summary contains at least one of: a player name, a score, a stat, or a specific date reference.
- Verify the regenerated summary does NOT contain phrases like "the team is preparing" or "players are working hard".

## Integration Tests

### 4. End-to-End Pipeline Run
- Run the `run-content-pipeline` Edge Function for a test team (e.g., Seattle Seahawks) with a mix of articles likely to produce generic and specific summaries.
- Query `articles` table and verify:
  - All articles have a non-null `summary`.
  - All articles have a non-null `is_generic_summary` boolean value.
  - Articles that were regenerated have `is_generic_summary = true`.

### 5. Database Column Verification
- Confirm `is_generic_summary` column exists on `articles` table with type `boolean` and default `false`.
- Insert a test article without setting `is_generic_summary` and verify it defaults to `false`.

### 6. Fallback Behavior
- Mock the Claude API to throw an error during the freshness check.
- Verify the pipeline continues without crashing and the original summary is preserved.
- Verify `is_generic_summary` defaults to `false` (not set) when the check fails.

### 7. Rate Limiting / Retry
- Simulate a high volume of articles (10+) and verify all are processed without hitting unhandled rate limit errors.
- Verify delays between API calls are respected.

## Monitoring / Observability
- After a full pipeline run, query: `SELECT COUNT(*), is_generic_summary FROM articles GROUP BY is_generic_summary;`
- Verify the ratio of generic summaries is reasonable (e.g., less than 20% flagged in a healthy run).
- Check Edge Function logs for `"Generic summary detected"` warnings and confirm they correspond to `is_generic_summary = true` rows in the DB.

tasks.title: Implement Summary Contradiction Check Against Article Headline
tasks.description: After generating an article summary, call the Claude API to verify the summary does not contradict or misrepresent the article's headline, regenerating the summary if a contradiction is detected. Store the result in a new `contradicts_headline` boolean column on the `articles` table.
tasks.testStrategy:
## 1. Database Migration Verification
- Run `SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'articles' AND column_name = 'contradicts_headline';` and confirm the column exists with type `boolean` and default `false`.

## 2. Unit Test — `checkSummaryContradiction` Helper
- **True contradiction**: Pass headline `"Seahawks QB Wilson ruled OUT for Sunday"` and summary `"Wilson is confirmed to start Sunday after a full week of practice."` — verify `contradicts: true` is returned.
- **No contradiction**: Pass headline `"Seahawks sign veteran wide receiver"` and summary `"Seattle added an experienced WR to bolster their receiving corps."` — verify `contradicts: false` is returned.
- **Malformed Claude response**: Mock the Claude API to return invalid JSON and verify the function gracefully defaults to `{ contradicts: false }` without throwing.

## 3. Integration Test — Regeneration Loop
- Mock the Claude summarization call to return a contradicting summary on the first call and a valid summary on the second call.
- Run `summarizeArticles` for a single article and verify:
  - The function made exactly 2 summarization calls.
  - The final `contradicts_headline` value stored in the `articles` table is `false`.
  - The article is included in the returned `summarized` array.

## 4. Integration Test — Max Attempts Exceeded
- Mock the Claude summarization call to always return a contradicting summary.
- Run `summarizeArticles` for a single article and verify:
  - Exactly `MAX_REGENERATION_ATTEMPTS + 1` (3) summarization calls were made.
  - `contradicts_headline = true` is stored in the `articles` table.
  - The article is **not** included in the returned `summarized` array.

## 5. End-to-End Pipeline Test
- Run the full `run-content-pipeline` for a test team.
- Query `SELECT id, title, summary, contradicts_headline FROM articles WHERE team_id = '<test_team_id>' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;`
- Verify all published articles have `contradicts_headline = false`.
- Manually inspect 3–5 summaries against their headlines to confirm accuracy.

## 6. Downstream Filtering Guard Test
- Manually set `contradicts_headline = true` for an article in the database.
- Run newsletter assembly (Task 26) and verify the flagged article does not appear in the assembled newsletter HTML or article list.

## 7. Logging Verification
- Trigger a contradiction scenario and verify warning/error log lines appear in the Supabase Edge Function logs with the article ID, attempt number, and reason string.

## 8. Null/Empty Headline Edge Case
- Insert a test article with a `NULL` or empty `title` and run the pipeline.
- Verify the contradiction check is skipped and `contradicts_headline` defaults to `false` without errors.

tasks.title: Enforce Summary Length, Tone Rules, and Version Tracking
tasks.description: Programmatically enforce a maximum 3-sentence limit on article summaries at sentence boundaries, flag summaries containing speculative language (may, could, might) unless those words appear in the original article, and store a `summary_version` integer that increments on each regeneration.
tasks.testStrategy:
## 1. Database Migration Verification

- Run `SELECT column_name, data_type, column_default FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'articles' AND column_name IN ('summary_version', 'speculative_language_flagged');`
- Confirm `summary_version` exists as `integer` with default `1`.
- Confirm `speculative_language_flagged` exists as `boolean` with default `false`.

---

## 2. Unit Tests — `enforceSentenceLimit`

### 2a. Exactly 3 sentences — no truncation
- Input: `"The Seahawks won. Geno Smith threw for 300 yards. The defense held firm."`
- Expected output: same string (unchanged).

### 2b. More than 3 sentences — truncate at boundary
- Input: `"The Seahawks won. Geno Smith threw for 300 yards. The defense held firm. Fans celebrated loudly."`
- Expected output: `"The Seahawks won. Geno Smith threw for 300 yards. The defense held firm."`
- Verify the 4th sentence `"Fans celebrated loudly."` is NOT present.

### 2c. Fewer than 3 sentences — no change
- Input: `"The Seahawks won on Sunday."`
- Expected output: same string.

### 2d. No sentence-ending punctuation
- Input: `"The Seahawks won on Sunday in a close game"`
- Expected output: same string (treated as one sentence).

### 2e. Exclamation and question marks as sentence boundaries
- Input: `"What a game! The Seahawks won. Can they keep it up? Fans are hopeful."`
- Expected output: `"What a game! The Seahawks won. Can they keep it up?"`

---

## 3. Unit Tests — `detectSpeculativeLanguage`

### 3a. Speculative word in summary but NOT in original — flagged
- Summary: `"The Seahawks may start a new quarterback next week."`
- Original: `"The Seahawks are evaluating their roster options."`
- Expected: `{ flagged: true, offendingWords: ['may'] }`

### 3b. Speculative word in summary AND in original — not flagged
- Summary: `"The Seahawks could make a trade before the deadline."`
- Original: `"Sources say the Seahawks could make a trade before the deadline."`
- Expected: `{ flagged: false, offendingWords: [] }`

### 3c. Multiple speculative words, mixed presence
- Summary: `"The team might trade Smith and could release Jones."`
- Original: `"The team might trade Smith."` (contains "might" but not "could")
- Expected: `{ flagged: true, offendingWords: ['could'] }`

### 3d. No speculative words — not flagged
- Summary: `"The Seahawks defeated the 49ers 24-17 on Sunday."`
- Original: `"The Seahawks defeated the 49ers 24-17."`
- Expected: `{ flagged: false, offendingWords: [] }`

### 3e. Whole-word matching — "couldn't" should not trigger "could"
- Summary: `"The offense couldn't find its rhythm."`
- Original: `"The offense struggled."`
- Expected: `{ flagged: false, offendingWords: [] }`

---

## 4. Integration Tests — `summary_version` Increment

### 4a. First-time generation
- Insert a new article with no prior `summary`. Run pipeline.
- Verify `summary_version = 1` in the `articles` table after upsert.

### 4b. Regeneration due to speculative language
- Mock Claude to return a summary with `"may"` not present in the original article.
- Run pipeline. Verify `summary_version = 2` after the regeneration cycle.

### 4c. No change on identical summary
- Run pipeline twice on the same article where Claude returns the same summary both times.
- Verify `summary_version` does NOT increment on the second run.

### 4d. Version persists across pipeline runs
- Set `summary_version = 3` manually in the DB for an article.
- Run pipeline with a new summary for that article.
- Verify `summary_version = 4` after the run.

---

## 5. End-to-End Pipeline Test

- Run the full `run-content-pipeline` Edge Function for a test team.
- Query `SELECT url, summary, summary_version, speculative_language_flagged FROM articles WHERE created_at > now() - interval '1 hour';`
- Verify:
  - All summaries have ≤ 3 sentences (count sentence-ending punctuation).
  - Any article with `speculative_language_flagged = true` has a `summary_version >= 2`.
  - No summary contains "may", "could", or "might" unless those words appear in the corresponding `raw_content`.
  - All `summary_version` values are ≥ 1 (never 0 or null).

tasks.title: Implement Satisfaction Feedback Loop with Article Logging, Weekly Admin Reports, and Auto-Flagging
tasks.description: When a thumbs-down feedback is received, log the articles selected that day along with their composite_scores and selection_reasoning. Generate a weekly admin report comparing lowest vs highest rated newsletters to surface pattern differences, and auto-flag the admin team for review if satisfaction rate drops below 60% for 3 consecutive issues.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Testing Strategy

### Component 1: Thumbs-Down Article Logging

1. **Happy path**: Submit a thumbs-down feedback for a newsletter that has 5 articles with known `composite_score` and `selection_reasoning` values. Query `feedback_article_logs` and verify:
   - A row is created with the correct `newsletter_id`, `subscriber_id`, and `team_id`.
   - The `articles` JSONB array contains all 5 articles with correct `composite_score`, `selection_reasoning`, `source_name`, and `rank` fields.
2. **Thumbs-up does NOT log**: Submit a thumbs-up feedback and verify no row is inserted into `feedback_article_logs`.
3. **Missing articles gracefully handled**: Submit thumbs-down for a newsletter with no articles in the DB; verify no crash and either an empty array or no row is inserted.
4. **Multiple thumbs-down from different subscribers**: Verify separate rows are created per subscriber for the same newsletter.

### Component 2: Weekly Admin Report

1. **Seed data**: Insert 6+ sent newsletters across 2+ teams with varying `thumbs_up_count` and `thumbs_down_count` in `newsletter_metrics`. Insert corresponding `feedback_article_logs` for the lowest-rated ones.
2. **Trigger the function manually** via HTTP POST to `weekly-satisfaction-report`.
3. **Verify email sent**: Check admin inbox for the weekly report email with correct subject line.
4. **Verify report content**:
   - Top 3 and bottom 3 newsletters are correctly identified by satisfaction rate.
   - Article breakdown appears for lowest-rated newsletters with composite_score and selection_reasoning.
   - Pattern differences section highlights articles with composite_score < 0.5.
5. **Verify `admin_reports` table**: Confirm a row is inserted with `report_type = 'weekly_satisfaction'` and non-empty `html_content`.
6. **Edge case – fewer than 3 rated newsletters**: Verify the report handles fewer than 3 newsletters gracefully (shows what's available without crashing).
7. **Cron schedule**: Verify the pg_cron job is registered by querying `SELECT * FROM cron.job WHERE jobname = 'weekly-satisfaction-report'`.

### Component 3: Auto-Flag for Consecutive Low Satisfaction

1. **3 consecutive below 60%**: Insert 3 sent newsletters for a team, each with satisfaction rate ~40% (e.g., 2 up / 3 down). Trigger the feedback function for the most recent one. Verify:
   - A row is inserted into `admin_flags` with `flag_type = 'low_satisfaction_streak'` and `resolved = false`.
   - Admin notification email is received with the correct team and newsletter IDs.
2. **Only 2 consecutive below 60%**: Insert 3 newsletters where the oldest has >60% satisfaction. Verify no flag is created.
3. **Deduplication**: Trigger the check twice within 7 days for the same team. Verify only one flag row is created (no duplicate alert).
4. **Insufficient history (< 3 newsletters)**: Verify no flag is created when a team has fewer than 3 sent newsletters.
5. **No feedback on newsletters**: Verify newsletters with 0 total feedback (thumbs_up + thumbs_down = 0) do not count as "below 60%" and do not trigger the flag.
6. **Flag resolution**: Manually set `resolved = true` on a flag and verify the next check for the same team (still below 60%) creates a new flag after the 7-day cooldown window.

### Integration Test

1. Simulate a full end-to-end flow: send a newsletter → subscriber clicks thumbs-down → verify article log created → repeat for 3 consecutive newsletters → verify admin flag created and email sent → trigger weekly report → verify report includes the flagged team's newsletters in the "lowest rated" section.

tasks.title: Implement Seasonal Mode Detection and Dynamic Scoring Weight Adjustment
tasks.description: Detect the current NFL calendar phase (preseason, regular_season, playoffs, offseason, draft_period) and dynamically adjust article scoring weights, composite thresholds, source filters, and uniqueness scoring to reflect the editorial priorities of each phase.
tasks.testStrategy:
## Unit Tests for `detectSeasonalMode()`

1. **Phase boundary correctness**: Call `detectSeasonalMode()` with mocked dates covering each phase boundary (e.g., Feb 1, Feb 15, Apr 20, May 11, Aug 1, Sep 6, Jan 11) and assert the correct `NFLSeasonPhase` is returned for each.
2. **Offseason fallback**: Pass a date in mid-March and verify `'offseason'` is returned.
3. **Super Bowl week priority**: Pass Feb 7 and verify `'super_bowl_week'` is returned (not `'playoffs'`).
4. **Manual override**: Set `SEASON_PHASE_OVERRIDE=draft_period` env var and verify `detectSeasonalMode()` returns `'draft_period'` regardless of the current date.

## Unit Tests for `getSeasonalConfig()`

5. **Offseason config**: Call `getSeasonalConfig('offseason')` and assert `significance` weight is reduced and `rumor` weight is elevated (≥ 1.5).
6. **Draft period threshold**: Call `getSeasonalConfig('draft_period')` and assert `compositeThresholdOverride === 55` and `transaction` weight is elevated.
7. **Playoffs source filter**: Call `getSeasonalConfig('playoffs')` and assert `globalSourceFilterTightened === true`.
8. **Super Bowl uniqueness**: Call `getSeasonalConfig('super_bowl_week')` and assert `uniquenessMode === 'spread'`.
9. **Regular season defaults**: Call `getSeasonalConfig('regular_season')` and assert no overrides are applied (all defaults).

## Integration Tests in Pipeline

10. **Offseason rumor elevation**: Seed test articles with `category='rumor'` and `category='general'`. Mock date to offseason. Run pipeline and verify rumor articles score higher than they would under regular season weights.
11. **Draft period threshold**: Seed articles with `composite_score` between 55–59. Mock date to draft period. Verify these articles pass the threshold filter (they would be excluded in regular season at threshold 60).
12. **Playoffs source filter**: Seed articles from tier-1 and tier-2 sources. Mock date to playoffs. Verify only tier-1 source articles remain after filtering.
13. **Super Bowl spread mode**: Seed 6 articles from the same source. Mock date to Super Bowl week. Run `rankAndSelectArticles` with `uniquenessMode='spread'` and verify the selected articles come from diverse sources/topics rather than all from the same source.
14. **Composite score recalculation**: Verify that `recalculateCompositeScore()` correctly applies weight multipliers and that the resulting scores differ from the pre-seasonal scores in the expected direction.

## Observability

15. **Logging**: Run the pipeline and verify the Supabase logs (or console output) contain the detected phase name and the applied config JSON.
16. **No regression on regular season**: Run the full pipeline with a regular season date and verify output matches pre-task behavior (no unintended score changes).

tasks.title: Implement Volume Throttling for Per-Source Article Fetching
tasks.description: Cap the number of articles fetched per source per day at 3 to prevent single-source dominance, and apply dynamic relevance score cutoff adjustments based on the total number of articles passing the threshold, with admin notifications for low-article scenarios.
tasks.testStrategy:
### 1. Per-Source Daily Cap (Max 3 Articles Per Source)

- Create a mock article set with 6 articles from Source A (varying composite_scores) and 2 from Source B.
- Run `applyPerSourceCap()` and verify only the top 3 articles from Source A are retained (by composite_score), and both from Source B pass through.
- Verify total output is 5 articles (3 from A + 2 from B).
- Confirm articles are sorted by composite_score within each source group.

### 2. Strict Threshold (> 10 Articles Pass Base)

- Create a mock capped article set where 12 articles have composite_score ≥ 65.
- Run `applyDynamicThreshold()` and verify the returned list only contains articles with composite_score ≥ 75.
- Verify no admin notification is sent in this scenario.

### 3. Lenient Threshold (< 3 Articles Pass Base)

- Create a mock capped article set where only 2 articles have composite_score ≥ 65, but 5 articles have composite_score ≥ 55.
- Run `applyDynamicThreshold()` and verify the returned list contains all articles with composite_score ≥ 55 (5 articles).
- Verify admin notification is triggered with the correct `low_article_count` type, team_id, and threshold values.
- Verify the notification does not block pipeline execution (use try/catch and confirm pipeline continues even if notify fails).

### 4. Normal Range (3–10 Articles Pass Base)

- Create a mock capped article set where 6 articles have composite_score ≥ 65.
- Run `applyDynamicThreshold()` and verify the returned list contains exactly those 6 articles (base threshold applied, no adjustment).
- Verify no admin notification is sent.

### 5. Pipeline Integration Order

- Run the full `run-content-pipeline` for a test team and add logging to confirm the execution order: scoring → per-source cap → dynamic threshold → duplicate suppression → rankAndSelectArticles.
- Verify no article from a single source appears more than 3 times in the pre-selection candidate pool.

### 6. Admin Notification Content

- Trigger the low-article-count scenario and verify the admin receives an email with the correct subject (`Low Article Count Alert: Team <id>`), team ID, base threshold, and lenient threshold values in the body.

### 7. Edge Cases

- Test with 0 articles after scoring: verify pipeline handles gracefully without errors and admin is notified.
- Test with exactly 3 articles passing base threshold: verify lenient threshold is NOT applied (boundary condition).
- Test with exactly 10 articles passing base threshold: verify strict threshold is NOT applied (boundary condition).

tasks.title: Implement Stat of the Day Grounding with Source Article Traceability
tasks.description: Refactor the Stat of the Day generation to derive statistics exclusively from fetched articles rather than freely generating them, storing a reference to the source article and validation notes from any web search used to verify the stat. If no verifiable stat can be found in today's articles, the Stat of the Day section is omitted entirely from the newsletter.
tasks.testStrategy:
## 1. Stat Grounding — Stat Extracted from Article

- Provide a mock set of articles where one article contains a clear numeric statistic (e.g., "The Seahawks have won 8 consecutive home games").
- Run `generateStatOfTheDay` and verify:
  - The returned `stat` text matches or paraphrases content from the provided articles.
  - The returned `source_article_id` matches the UUID of the article containing the stat.
  - The stat is NOT a freely invented claim absent from the article corpus.

## 2. Stat Grounding — No Stat Available

- Provide a mock set of articles containing only general narrative text with no specific statistics.
- Run `generateStatOfTheDay` and verify it returns `null`.
- Run the full newsletter assembly and verify the assembled HTML does **not** contain a "Stat of the Day" section (no empty `<section>` tag, no placeholder text).

## 3. `stat_source_article_id` Persistence

- Run the full pipeline for a test team with articles that contain a verifiable stat.
- Query the `newsletters` table after assembly and verify:
  - `stat_source_article_id` is non-null and references a valid row in the `articles` table.
  - `stat_validation_notes` is non-null and contains a meaningful string.

## 4. `stat_source_article_id` Null When No Stat

- Run the pipeline with articles containing no stats.
- Query the `newsletters` table and verify `stat_source_article_id IS NULL` and `stat_validation_notes IS NULL`.

## 5. Web Search Verification — Numeric Stat

- Mock `performWebSearch` to return a known snippet.
- Call `verifyStatWithWebSearch` with a stat containing digits.
- Verify `validation_notes` contains the search query and the mocked snippet URL.

## 6. Web Search Verification — Non-Numeric Stat

- Call `verifyStatWithWebSearch` with a stat containing no digits (e.g., "The Seahawks have the best offensive line in the NFC West").
- Verify `validation_notes` contains the "No web verification performed" message and no web search API call was made.

## 7. Hallucinated Article ID Rejection

- Modify the Claude mock response to return a `source_article_id` that does not exist in the provided article list.
- Verify `generateStatOfTheDay` returns `null` (rejects the hallucinated ID).

## 8. Database Schema Validation

- Run the migration SQL and verify the `newsletters` table has `stat_source_article_id` (uuid, nullable, FK to articles) and `stat_validation_notes` (text, nullable) columns.
- Attempt to insert a `stat_source_article_id` referencing a non-existent article UUID and verify the FK constraint rejects it.

## 9. Admin Notification on No Stat Found

- Configure the pipeline with articles containing no stats.
- Verify an admin warning log entry is created (or admin notification sent via Task 38's system) indicating the Stat of the Day section was omitted.

## 10. End-to-End Newsletter Rendering

- Run the full pipeline for a team with stat-containing articles and verify the rendered HTML email includes the Stat of the Day section with correct content.
- Run for a team with no stat-containing articles and verify the section is completely absent from the HTML output.
